title: Canthium infaustum
intro: Canthium infaustum, also known as the African mistletoe, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Canthium infaustum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Canthium infaustum include tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions, fever, and other infections.
taxonomy: Canthium infaustum is a plant species classified under the family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, comprises about 600 genera and over 13,000 species. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species within the Rubiaceae family.
